name: Puree of Garlic Soup with Chicken Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Garlic (about 8 medium garlic heads, separated into cloves but unpeeled)
2 cups Mixed diced vegetables (such as zucchini, yellow squash, eggplant and green beans)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare Garlic Soup: Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a 2-quart pan over medium heat. Add garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until skins start to turn golden, about 15 to 20 minutes.
2. Add onions and cook until soft, another 5 minutes. Add broth, lemon, bay leaves, and thyme. Increase heat to high and bring to boil, then lower heat to medium and simmer for 30 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, heat remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil in a small pan over medium heat. Add vegetables. Cover and cook until soft, about 10 minutes. Set aside.
4. Prepare Chicken Dumplings: Cut chicken into 2-ounce pieces and place in food processor along with minced garlic, salt, ground white pepper, Madeira or dry sherry, and egg whites. Pulse on and off until mixture is coarsely chopped. Scrape into a bowl. Add finely diced onion and mix well.
5. Remove soup from heat. Discard bay leaves and lemon. Puree the mixture in a food processor or blender. Strain the soup through a strainer or pass it through a food mill into a clean pan. Return to a simmer.
6. Use a spoon to drop dollops of Chicken Dumplings into the simmering garlic soup, creating 12 dumplings. Cover and cook for 8 minutes.
7. Ladle soup into serving bowls at the table. Garnish with reserved vegetables and toasted bread.
